h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’ve ticked off slope, load, surface, and edge strip, so I was hoping this was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, my checked-boxes-for-sign-off line was too simple; it hid whether the ramp still makes the person solve the sideways entry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply execute the physical test and record the binary outcomes of the four failure modes provided by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Make “small” visible. Watch whether the ramp causes the change: wheel angle, pause, lift, widened turn, hand signal.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's anxiety about subjective judgment by shifting the evidence standard from a vague 'small correction' to concrete, observable physical changes.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Make “small” visible. Watch whether the ramp causes the change: wheel angle, pause, lift, widened turn, hand signal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's newly articulated hesitation about defining a 'small' correction by providing a specific set of observable physical indicators.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I’ve ticked off slope, load, surface, and edge strip, so I was hoping this was ready for sign-off.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Make “small” visible. Watch whether the ramp causes the change: wheel angle, pause, lift, widened turn, hand signal.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's newly articulated hesitation about defining a 'small' correction by providing a specific set of observable physical indicators.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Make “small” visible. Watch whether the ramp causes the change: wheel angle, pause, lift, widened turn, hand signal.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's anxiety about subjective judgment by shifting the evidence standard from a vague 'small correction' to concrete, observable physical changes.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Make “small” visible. Watch whether the ramp causes the change: wheel angle, pause, lift, widened turn, hand signal.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's anxiety about subjective judgment by shifting the evidence standard from a vague 'small correction' to concrete, observable physical changes.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled this side, so I still want to say the square got twice as large.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: “Double this side, double the area” was too fast.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner intends to find a way to justify their initial intuition that doubling the side doubles the area.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: First hold the old height: two across, one up. How many boxes?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from abstract correction to a concrete physical manipulation of the ruler to expose the hidden dimension.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Now make the portfolio line do the same work: side length times 2 along the base, times 2 up the edge; area times 2 times 2.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adapts the task to have the learner rewrite their portfolio sentence to reflect the newly recognized two-dimensional scaling.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled this side, so I still want to say the square got twice as large.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Now make the portfolio line do the same work: side length times 2 along the base, times 2 up the edge; area times 2 times 2.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor adapts the task to have the learner rewrite their portfolio sentence to reflect the newly recognized two-dimensional scaling.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: First hold the old height: two across, one up. How many boxes?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from abstract correction to a concrete physical manipulation of the ruler to expose the hidden dimension.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: First hold the old height: two across, one up. How many boxes?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from abstract correction to a concrete physical manipulation of the ruler to expose the hidden dimension.
